sections.subtitle: Team Form Analysis
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés is in poor form, with no wins and no goals scored in their last 3 matches. Levante UD, on the other hand, has shown better attacking intent, winning 3 of their last 5 matches and scoring in 4 of them.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: The last two head-to-head matches between these teams ended in draws (1-1 and 0-0), indicating tight contests. Matches between them often produce fewer goals, with under 2.5 goals in all recent encounters.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Trends
sections.text: Both teams have seen low-scoring games recently, with under 2.5 goals in most of their matches. Corner kicks tend to be high, with over 10.5 corners in several recent games for both sides, while yellow cards are usually under 4.5 per game.

sections.subtitle: Key Players Impact
sections.text: Levante UD's in-form players like Roger Brugué (4 goals in 5 matches) could exploit Alavés' defensive struggles. Alavés will rely on defensive solidity, with players like Nahuel Tenaglia performing consistently.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Deportivo Alavés take a narrow lead over Levante UD with a score of 1-0 at Estadio de Mendizorroza. Alavés controlled much of the game, dominating possession and creating more opportunities in the final third. Their persistence paid off with a well-taken header by Toni Martínez in the 36th minute, showcasing their ability to capitalize on set-piece situations. Levante, on the other hand, struggled to create meaningful chances, often finding themselves on the back foot defensively. The home side's dominance in duels and aerial challenges further highlighted their control over the match's tempo.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, Deportivo Alavés displayed consistent attacking intent with frequent spells of pressure in the opposition's half, particularly in key moments leading up to their goal. Their momentum shifted positively in several phases, especially in the latter stages of the half, where they maintained sustained attacks. Levante UD had their moments of pushing forward, with brief periods of momentum early on and around the middle of the half, but their attacking spells were less frequent and lacked the necessary sharpness to truly threaten Alavés' defense. Overall, the home side dictated the rhythm of the game offensively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performances
halftimeSections.text: For Deportivo Alavés, Toni Martínez stood out with his clinical finishing, securing the only goal of the half and proving to be a constant threat upfront. Carles Aleñá also played a pivotal role, providing the assist for the goal and creating dangerous opportunities with his vision and passing. On the defensive end, players like Antonio Blanco and Nahuel Tenaglia were solid, contributing with crucial clearances and interceptions. For Levante UD, Iván Romero showed some intent with a shot on target, but lacked support in attack. Their defense, marshaled by Unai Elguezabal with multiple clearances, managed to limit further damage but struggled under Alavés' pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés effectively utilized their superior possession to control the game, frequently entering the penalty area and earning several corner kicks to maintain pressure. Their approach in the final third was more structured, focusing on creating big chances, as evidenced by their goal. Levante UD, in contrast, appeared more reactive, with fewer entries into the final third and limited success in winning duels. Their lower possession share forced them into a more defensive posture, and they will need to adjust their strategy in the second half to create more attacking opportunities and regain some control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: For Alavés, the focus should be on maintaining their intensity and converting more of their final third entries into clear scoring opportunities. While they’ve been dominant, they’ll want to avoid complacency against a Levante side that may push harder after the break. Levante UD needs to improve their ball retention and work on transitioning quicker into attack. Their lack of big chances created in the first half is a concern, and they must find ways to win more duels in midfield to disrupt Alavés’ rhythm and create space for their forwards.
